items.description: Delta Air Lines CEO Ed Bastian says oil prices will stay "sticky for longer," but the carrier will still do "just fine." He says there is still strong demand for premium and international travel. He s

items.title: We Tried Out Dyson's New Portable Fan
items.description: The newest status symbol on the streets of Manhattan is a high-tech portable fan that’s been hard to get hold of. Samantha Kelly tried one of Dyson's new portable fans out in the New York City heat.

items.title: Delta Will Do Just Fine With High Fuel Prices, CEO Says
items.description: Delta Air Lines Chief Executive Officer Ed Bastian says higher fuel prices are not deterring travel. He says the carrier is actually seeing strong demand for premium, corporate and international trave

items.title: Cocoa’s Uneven Demand Recovery Runs Into Fresh Price Volatility
items.description: Cocoa demand is expected to remain uneven across the world, suggesting the recovery remains fragile as renewed supply concerns push bean prices higher and threaten to keep chocolate pricier for longer

items.title: Shein Is Said to Target Up to $3 Billion Hong Kong IPO by August
items.description: Shein Global Holdings Ltd. is seeking to list in Hong Kong as soon as in August after securing approval from China’s securities regulator, according to people familiar with the matter.

items.title: Saylor’s Strategy Raises Reserve to $3 Billion With Stock Sale
items.description: Michael Saylor’s Strategy Inc. raised about $467 million through the sale of common stock, lifting its cash reserve to roughly $3 billion while refraining from buying or selling Bitcoin.

items.title: The C-Beauty Industry’s Unique Advantage
items.description: As Korean entertainment helped turn K-beauty into a global phenomenon, China's growing influence in popular culture could do the same for its cosmetics industry, Bloomberg Opinion columnist Juliana Li

items.title: Kenya Eyes $500 Million Eurobond Buyback to Ease Debt Pressure
items.description: Kenya is considering buying back as much as $500 million of its eurobonds to extend maturities and ease repayment pressures, according to people familiar with the plans.

items.title: Disney Exiting Streaming Could Spur 40% Rally, Wells Fargo Says
items.description: Walt Disney Co. shares have been in a slump for years, but Wells Fargo Securities said there’s one possible move that could reverse the trend: ditching its streaming-video business.

items.title: VW CEO Outlines Up to 50,000 More Job Cuts to Hit Savings Goals
items.description: Volkswagen AG Chief Executive Officer Oliver Blume outlined a plan to eliminate as many as 50,000 more jobs globally as he attempts a far-reaching overhaul to reduce costs at Europe’s biggest carmaker

items.title: US Stocks Fall as Mideast Tensions Flare Anew, Oil Climbs
items.description: US stocks dropped on Monday as investors assessed tit-for-tat attacks between the US and Iran and conflicting information over the status of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Oil Rises as US, Iran Dispute Whether Hormuz is Open; Big Week in Markets | Bloomberg Brief 07/13/26
items.description: Oil gains after the US and Iran trade fresh strikes. The two sides are issuing conflicting declarations whether the Strait of Hormuz is open. Investors prepare for Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh's

items.title: Wall Street Mounts Pushback on Trillion-Dollar Stablecoin Boom

items.title: Russian Refinery Runs Plunge to Lowest in More Than Two Decades
items.description: A wave of Ukrainian attacks has pushed Russian refining runs to the lowest in more than 21 years, deepening a domestic fuel crunch and further squeezing the global market.

items.title: Bangladesh Is a Nuclear Power Test Case for Developing Nations
items.description: Atomic energy offers emerging economies a way to wean themselves off imported fossil fuels, but it also has downsides.

items.title: Saudi Energy Minister’s Influence Grows With Industry Portfolio

items.title: Renewed Iran Woes Flip Script for Traders Betting War Risks Over
items.description: A day after making an investment call tied to subsiding Middle East tensions, BCA Research’s Marko Papic had to scrap his recommendation at a loss. Donald Trump had said the war in Iran was back on.

items.title: Chile Debt Sale Window Opens as Investors Ignore Mounting Risks
items.description: Chile’s government has a window of opportunity to sell debt abroad as sovereign spreads hover near two-decade lows, with investors shrugging off an escalation in the Middle East conflict, domestic deb

items.title: Investors Expect ‘Sunshine and Rainbows’ This Earnings Season
items.description: Estimates are running up against sticky inflation and rising odds of interest rate hikes. With stocks near records, there’s little room for error.
